title: Client Inventory and Connection Summary
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:client`. from the `client` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per client per poll (can be very high volume). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `macAddress` (string), `hostType` (string), `connectionType` (string), `healthScore{}` (object), `ssid` (string), `vlanId` (string), `location` (string).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" | stats dc(macAddress) as unique_clients count as total_records latest(connectionType) as connection_type by hostType | sort -unique_clients
description: Provides a complete inventory of all clients detected by Catalyst Center, summarized by host type and connection method.
value: Understanding your client population (count, types, connection methods) is foundational for capacity planning and security policy design.
implementation: Enable the `client` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA calls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail` on a **3600s** (1 hour) default interval. Key fields: `macAddress`, `hostType`, `connectionType`, `ssid`, `vlanId`, `healthScore` (and `location` when present).
visualization: Table (host type, count, connection type), Pie chart (client types), Single value (total clients).

references.title: Catalyst Center Client Detail API ‚Äî Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives: The TA‚Äôs poll interval (often an hour) misses short-lived **guest** or **transient** clients, so `unique_clients` can look ‚Äútoo low‚Äù versus the live Catalyst UI. **Count** and **dc(macAddress)** differ when the add-on **emits** multiple **rows** per **MAC** per run (e.g. history segments); the summary still answers ‚Äúwhat‚Äôs on the **network** in aggregate‚Äù without being a second-by-second **headcount**.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538); **client** feed into `index=catalyst`, sourcetype `cisco:dnac:client`.
‚Ä¢ Catalyst **Assurance** / client visibility licensed where your organization relies on **Client 360**-style data; **API** user with read access to **client detail** in your **Intent** **RBAC** model.
‚Ä¢ **PII** / **data-classification** review: **MAC addresses** and **hostnames** may be sensitive‚Äîrestrict **index** **ACLs** and **dashboard** audiences per policy.
‚Ä¢ `docs/implementation-guide.md` for modular input **interval**, **scope** (global vs site), and **proxy** settings.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail` (client inventory and health detail as implemented in your **Catalyst** and **TA** version).
‚Ä¢ **TA input name:** **client**; sourcetype `cisco:dnac:client`.
‚Ä¢ **Default interval:** **3600 seconds (1 hour)**‚Äîlarger sites may need **tuning**; shorter intervals **increase** **API** load; align with how often **NOC** expects **roaming** updates.
‚Ä¢ **Key fields:** **`macAddress`**, **`hostType`**, **`connectionType`**, **`ssid`**, **`vlanId`**, **`healthScore`** (plus **`location`** or site fields if your **TA** enriches them).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" | stats dc(macAddress) as unique_clients count as total_records latest(connectionType) as connection_type by hostType | sort -unique_clients
```

Understanding this SPL
‚Ä¢ **`dc(macAddress)`** counts **distinct** clients by **`hostType`**‚Äîif one **MAC** appears as **multiple** **record types** per poll, validate **dedup** rules with a **`values(macAddress)`** sample.
‚Ä¢ **`latest(connectionType)`** is a **simplification** for a **summary** table; for **per-SSID** cut, pivot with **`by ssid`** in a **separate** panel (see **UC-5.13.12** for health-by-segment context).

**Pipeline walkthrough**
‚Ä¢ Scopes the **client** feed; **aggregates** by **host** category for **capacity** and **security** ‚Äúwhat is on my network‚Äù views.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ Compare **rough order-of-magnitude** **unique** **Wi-Fi** vs **wired** clients to **Catalyst > Clients** for the same **time** (not byte-for-byte‚Äî**Catalyst** may **de-duplicate** differently than this **1h** **Splunk** **window**).
‚Ä¢ **Spot-check** a few **MACs** in **Catalyst** **Client 360** and confirm **`hostType`**, **`ssid`**, **`vlanId`** look consistent with **one** **raw** **Splunk** event.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ **Dashboard:** **table** of **hostType** with **unique_clients**; **single value** for **sum(unique_clients)** in a **subsearch** or **post-process**; optional **export** to **CMDB** **sync** (batch only‚Äîdo not **reverse**-sync **PII** without **governance**).
‚Ä¢ **Not** a real-time **location** system‚Äî**Assurance** **and** **Splunk** **latency** apply; use for **trends**, not **sub-minute** **tracking**.

‚Ä¢ **Validate:** compare against the Catalyst Center Assurance Client Health detail view ‚Äî verify per-client connection status and health scores.
Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **Zero events:** **client** **input** disabled, **wrong** **index**, or **API** **403**‚Äîcheck **TA** **logs** and **Catalyst** **roles**.
‚Ä¢ **Counts** **too** **high** vs **UI**:** **duplicates** per **poll**‚Äîadd a `dedup macAddress` (or equivalent) before `stats` if your **TA** emits multiple rows per **MAC** per poll.
‚Ä¢ **Missing** **SSID**/**VLAN**:** some **Wired** clients will **naturally** **lack** them‚Äî**filter** `connectionType` in **SPL** for **wireless**-only **panels**.
‚Ä¢ **PII** **escalation:** if **hostnames** appear in **raw** **JSON** but **should not** be **searched** broadly, **mask** in **ingest** or use **field-level** **restrictions** in **roles**.
‚Ä¢ **No client events arriving:** verify the `client` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
‚Ä¢ **NULL or zero values in health scores:** some per-client fields (RSSI, SNR) are only populated for wireless clients; empty values for wired clients are expected.
‚Ä¢ **More events than expected:** the client API generates one event per connected client per poll; if credential access is denied, check the service account role.
grandmaExplanation: We count and categorize every device connected to your network ‚Äî laptops, phones, printers, IoT ‚Äî so you know exactly what is out there.
